pi_items.source: Verified in task.json line 17, setup_task.sh line 18, export_result.sh line 18, and verifier.py line 43. Web search confirms this is a real address in Springfield, Illinois (https://www.foreclosure.com/listings/456-oak-avenue-springfield-state-illinois-62704/short-sales/)

Verified in task.json line 18, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh line 18, and verifier.py line 44. Springfield is a real city in Illinois (state capital). Confirmed as primary city for zip code 62704 (https://www.city-data.com/zips/62704.html)
Verified in task.json line 19, verifier.py line 45. IL is the correct state abbreviation for Illinois. Confirmed consistent across all setup scripts.

Verified in task.json line 20, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh line 18, and verifier.py line 46. Web search confirms 62704 is a real zip code for Springfield, Illinois. Part of Sangamon County, covers central Springfield area (https://www.city-data.com/zips/62704.html)

Verified in task.json line 21, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh line 18, and verifier.py line 47. Area code 217 is the real area code for Springfield, Illinois (established 1947, covers central Illinois). However, the 555 prefix is reserved for fictional telephone numbers in North American media and examples (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/555_(telephone_number)). The format is consistent across all files but uses the conventional fictional number prefix.

Verified in task.json line 22, setup_task.sh lines 30-35, export_result.sh line 18, and verifier.py line 51. This is a standard template/placeholder address format (123 Main Street is commonly used as a placeholder in documentation, legal forms, and examples per https://www.ccdbr.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/02/FOIA-Templates1.pdf)
Verified in task.json line 23, setup_task.sh lines 30-35, and verifier.py line 52. 'City' is clearly a template/placeholder value (not a real city name), intentionally generic for testing purposes.

Verified in task.json line 24, setup_task.sh lines 30-35, and verifier.py line 53. 'ST' is clearly a template/placeholder value (not a real state abbreviation), intentionally used as a generic placeholder in test data.

Verified in task.json line 25, setup_task.sh lines 30-35, and verifier.py line 54. 12345 is the standard sequential placeholder used in examples and test data (https://www.cliffsnotes.com/study-notes/27847556)

Verified in task.json line 26, setup_task.sh lines 30-35, and verifier.py line 55. This is a fictional phone number using the 555 prefix which is reserved for use in fiction, television, films, and examples in North America and is not assigned to real telephone service (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/555_(telephone_number))
Verified in setup_task.sh line 35 'WHERE id=1', direct_db_setup.sh line 93 'VALUES (1, ...)', and export_result.sh line 15 'WHERE id=1'. The task operates on schools table with id=1.
Verified in direct_db_setup.sh line 93 where school is created with title='Demo School', and task.json description mentions 'Demo School'

pi_items.metadata_value: Synthetic test data (not from real OpenSIS installations)
pi_items.source: All data is hardcoded in setup scripts and not loaded from external sources. Initial values are template placeholders (123 Main St, City, ST, 12345, 555-1234). Expected values use realistic locations (Springfield, IL 62704) but are also hardcoded for testing, not from real school operations.

privileged_info_summary: The update_school_info task operates on a synthetic 'Demo School' record (id=1) in OpenSIS v9.2. The initial school information uses template placeholders: '123 Main St' (template address), 'City' (template city name), 'ST' (template state code), '12345' (template zip code), and '555-1234' (fictional phone number with 555 prefix reserved for examples). The expected updated values are: '456 Oak Avenue' (a real address in Springfield, Illinois), 'Springfield' (real city), 'IL' (correct Illinois abbreviation), '62704' (real Springfield zip code), and '217-555-9876' (using real area code 217 for Springfield but with fictional 555 prefix). All data is synthetic, hardcoded in setup scripts, and not derived from real school operations or external data sources.
